sprints.name: Polish & Quick Wins
sprints.rationale: P1 items and fast visual wins — closes open UX gaps and knocks out the QA backlog before hardening begins.

items.title: Unified Notification Hub — Merge NotificationBell + TaskPanel
items.notes:
Current fragmentation: NotificationBell (top right), TaskPanel (processing queue), inline toasts, email queue. Create unified notification center with categories: Actions Needed (approvals, requests), System Events (audits complete), Alerts (score drops). Add notification preferences per workspace.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] NotificationBell and TaskPanel both ship as separate components rendered side-by-side; no unified hub merges their categories.

items.title: CMS Editor UX Redesign — Match SeoEditor simplicity
items.notes:
CmsEditor UI/UX is noticeably rougher than the clean static-page SeoEditor. Redesign to match: consistent card layout, inline field editing, clear save/publish flow, per-collection grouping with search/filter. Consider shared editing primitives between CmsEditor and SeoEditor to prevent future drift.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] CmsEditor.tsx already shares SeoEditor primitives (CharacterCounter, SerpPreview, SocialPreview, StatusBadge, statusBorderClass) and the PendingApprovals panel; full layout-parity redesign vs SeoEditor is unverified.

items.title: Stripe payment modal — visual QA in test mode (light + dark)
items.notes: Superseded 2026-05-04 by platform-consolidation-billing-checkout-only. The inline Stripe Elements payment modal and StripePaymentForm were removed; client purchases now redirect to hosted Stripe Checkout, so there is no in-app Elements modal to visually QA.

items.title: Mobile breakpoint visual smoke — Phase 5 token migration
items.source: Phase 5 Phase 2 follow-up — staging smoke gap
items.notes: Phase 2 codemod batches touched ~225 components but the original staging smoke test on 2026-04-25 only exercised desktop breakpoints — the resize_window MCP call didn't take effect on the Chrome instance. Tailwind responsive utilities are unchanged at the codemod level (only color tokens swapped, not breakpoint prefixes), but token swaps at breakpoint-conditional className boundaries could have edge cases. Test at 375 (mobile), 640 (sm), 768 (md), 1024 (lg) breakpoints across: WorkspaceOverview (admin home), ClientDashboard (overview/strategy/content tabs), SeoAudit (audit results table), Settings (Workspace + Client tabs). Look for: stacked vs grid layouts, button sizes, table overflow, sidebar collapse. Risk if skipped: medium — a regression at one breakpoint is invisible on desktop testing.

items.title: Harden flaky brand-engine + brand-identity integration tests
items.source: CI flakiness — recurring on staging push runs
items.notes: Fixed with a four-layer defense: (1) waitForServer health-check polling added to startServer() in tests/integration/helpers.ts so tests never fire before routes are serving; (2) SQLite busy_timeout increased from 5s to 30s to handle macOS APFS fsync stalls under parallel load; (3) incrementIfAllowed moved inside try/catch in all async AI handlers (brand-identity.ts, voice-calibration.ts) to prevent SQLITE_BUSY from escaping as an unhandled promise rejection that crashes the server; (4) global JSON error handler added to app.ts so synchronous throws return JSON 500 instead of HTML. All write transactions in voice-calibration.ts changed to .immediate() to prevent SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT in WAL mode. Pre-commit hook scoped to unit tests only to avoid parallel SQLite contention during local commits.

items.title: Schema: Write generated schema to CMS collection field
items.notes: Implemented first pass: collection-aware siteInventory, CMS collection role inference/mapping, synthetic CMS page IDs in single-page generation, utility-page bulk exclusions, diagnostics with collection identity/evidence/CMS delivery status, and JSON-only CMS field publishing through mapped collection fields such as schema-json-ld. Follow-up quality pass added persisted Detect + Map bindings for location/service fields, human-readable reference resolution, Service.offers from verified price/currency, safer homepage LocalBusiness gating, FAQ index suppression, and clearer schema-vs-CMS diagnostics. Remaining polish: broader collection role test coverage and deeper Webflow reference dereferencing where the API only returns IDs.

items.title: Form Validation Standardization — React Hook Form + Zod
items.notes: Replace basic HTML validation with consistent form library. Inline field-level validation with clear errors. aria-invalid + aria-describedby support. Success states for valid fields.

items.title: Loading State Consistency — Unified skeleton system
items.notes:
Standardize loading patterns: route-level (keep ChunkFallback), data fetching (skeleton screens), actions (button loading + optimistic UI), background jobs (progress panel).
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Skeleton primitive widely adopted (169 usages across 34 files including Roadmap, ClientDashboard, AdminInbox, brand/*, client/*); no formal standardization rule, lint, or codified loading-pattern doc.
Shipped / re-audited 2026-05-06: loading-state standardization now exists through shared Skeleton, LoadingState, SectionCardSkeleton, and ProgressIndicator primitives; route-level ChunkFallback remains in App.tsx, background jobs use TaskPanel/useBackgroundTasks, and docs/rules/ui-ux-consistency.md codifies contextual loading and skeleton usage. Remaining future polish can be tracked as specific component migrations rather than this broad standardization item.

items.title: Blog/content editor improvements — TipTap rich text, auto-save, reading time, AI Fix This flow
items.notes: Shipped — Phase 1: replaced all PostEditor/SectionEditor/PostReviewCard textareas with TipTap v3 rich text editor (bold/italic/H2/H3/links via BubbleMenu). New useAutoSave hook (debounced 2s, stale-closure-safe). Reading time added to PostPreview. Phase 2: AI 'Fix This' button on failed ReviewChecklist items. POST /api/content-posts/:wsId/:postId/ai-fix with 6 issue-key strategies (factual_accuracy, brand_voice, internal_links, no_hallucinations, meta_optimized, word_count_target). FixDiffModal shows two-column before/after diff. AiFixResult + IssueKey shared types. POST_UPDATED broadcast added to PATCH handler. 6 unit tests (useAutoSave) + 6 integration tests (ai-fix endpoint).

items.title: CI runtime optimization — path-filtered jobs, concurrency cancellation, and Playwright cache
items.notes: Shipped in .github/workflows/ci.yml and .github/workflows/e2e.yml. Added workflow-level concurrency cancel-in-progress to avoid duplicate runs per branch/PR. Split CI into change-detection + conditional quality/test/coverage jobs, moved coverage off PRs to push-only, and added required-check aggregation so branch protections stay stable. Added file-change gating via dorny/paths-filter for both CI and E2E so unchanged docs/non-runtime edits skip heavy jobs. Added Playwright browser cache keyed by Playwright version to avoid re-downloading browsers on every run. Follow-up optimization: E2E now runs in 2 Playwright shards (`--shard=1/2` and `--shard=2/2`) with a stable top-level `e2e` aggregator check so branch protection wiring stays unchanged. Additional follow-up: E2E now builds frontend once (`e2e-build` artifact) and reuses the shared `dist/` artifact in shards, and shard blob outputs are merged into a single `playwright-report-merged` artifact for easier debugging. Additional CI follow-up: PR Vitest execution now runs as a 3-way shard matrix (`--shard=1/3`, `2/3`, `3/3`) with a stable top-level `test` aggregator check to preserve required-check naming while reducing test wall-clock time.

sprints.name: Hardening Pass
sprints.rationale: Data normalization, validation coverage, test gaps, and small code-quality fixes that reduce future bug surface.

items.title: Content Post — Statistics Provenance Check
items.notes: Two-part improvement to reduce hallucinated statistics in generated content. (1) Brief-grounding constraint: during brief generation, extract and tag specific statistics found in scraped SERP/competitor/knowledge-base sources as verifiedStats[]. Inject a generation-time instruction to only cite numbers present in the brief research; use directional language ('significantly faster') when no sourced number exists. (2) Post-generation provenance flag: after unification pass, regex-match all numeric claims in the output and cross-reference against the brief research text. Any number not traceable to a source gets flagged in the PostEditor review checklist for human verification before publish. No automated fact-checking; the goal is surfacing the risk to the reviewer, not replacing judgment.

Future candidate is a narrow read-only PagePerformanceProfile assembler once at least two backend consumers need page + keyword + analytics in one response.

items.title: Shared WebSocket event bus — eliminate per-hook WS connections
items.source: Platform Health Sprint PR1 Devin review (2026-04-14)
items.notes: useWorkspaceEvents() creates a new WebSocket connection per call. SeoEditor + SeoAudit (and other multi-hook components) each open dedicated WS connections, scaling linearly with component count. Fix: lift workspace WS connection to a shared React context (or use the existing event bus pattern) so all hooks share one connection per workspaceId. Not urgent — connections close on unmount, but it's wasteful and could cause scaling issues with many concurrent users.

items.title: Page Rewriter: applyToSection feedback when target heading not found

items.notes:
extractPageSections() only tokenizes and

tags. Pages using

wrappers for body text (common in Webflow CMS templates) produce sections with empty body fields. Expand the two-pass tokenizer to also capture

items.title: JSON validation follow-up: schema coverage for high-value parseJsonFallback columns
items.notes: Three deferred items from typed-contracts PR (#87) code review: (1) work-orders.ts has bare JSON.parse calls not migrated in the PR — add parseJsonFallback or parseJsonSafeArray. (2) recommendations.ts and reports.ts use parseJsonFallback for high-value recommendation/summary data — add Zod schemas (RecommendationSchema, SummarySchema) and switch to parseJsonSafe with typed fallback. (3) pageKeywordMapSchema missing ~16 optional fields from PageKeywordMap interface — harmless due to .passthrough() but represents schema drift; add optional fields to keep schema in sync for future required-field additions.

items.title: Per-feature usage budgets (split strategy_generations pool)
items.source: PR #255 code review — Devin comment on shared billing pool
items.notes: All AI generation endpoints (keyword strategy, brand voice, knowledge base, personas, alt-text, brand identity deliverables) share a single 'strategy_generations' pool (free=0, growth=3, premium=∞). A user who generates a knowledge base + brand voice + personas exhausts their limit and can no longer generate alt text or deliverables — surprising UX. Options: (1) split into named sub-pools per feature category (e.g. 'brand_generations', 'seo_generations') each with their own limits; (2) raise the shared pool limit; (3) tier-specific per-feature overrides. Requires migration to add new usage_tracking feature keys, updating checkUsageLimit/incrementUsage call sites across all guarded routes, and updating client-facing usage display (if any). Coordinate with MONETIZATION.md tier redesign if limits are revisited.

items.title: Remove applyScoreAdjustment return-type escape hatches
items.notes:
anomaly-detection.ts:639 and outcome-tracking.ts:329 use `data: newData as never` because applyScoreAdjustment returns Partial<AnalyticsInsight> which doesn't overlap with the narrow discriminated union of InsightDataMap. Fix by making applyScoreAdjustment generic over InsightType so it preserves the data shape through the score adjustment. Blocked on #580.
Shipped / verified 2026-05-06: applyScoreAdjustment is generic over T extends Record<string, unknown> and anomaly-detection.ts/outcome-tracking.ts no longer use `data: newData as never` at the bridge call sites.

items.title: Refactor detectCompetitorAlerts: separate computation from DB persistence
items.source: TECH DEBT — scaled code review on PR #267 (feat/seo-tier2-capabilities)
items.notes: detectCompetitorAlerts() in server/competitor-snapshot-store.ts both computes alerts AND inserts them into the competitor_alerts table as a side effect. This tight coupling makes the function hard to unit-test in isolation and violates the separation seen elsewhere (e.g. computePageHealthScores returns data without DB writes). Fix: split into computeCompetitorAlerts(current, previous, opts) returning CompetitorAlert[] (pure, no DB writes) and saveCompetitorAlerts(workspaceId, alerts) for the insert pass. Callers in intelligence-crons.ts call both in sequence.

KeywordStrategyPanel starts the existing keyword-strategy job through useBackgroundTasks instead of direct SSE; content post generation is job-backed with step progress and cancellation; schema/page-analysis cancellation stays durable after abort-controller cleanup; SEO bulk workers unregister abort controllers; shared schema generation context prep is extracted; schema/page-analysis worker bodies are extracted from server/routes/jobs.ts into dedicated worker modules while the route remains the dispatcher; PageIntelligence rediscovers active page-analysis jobs after navigation/reload so inline progress derives from the same TaskPanel job state; schema snapshot generation, publish, rollback, retract, and delete paths now broadcast schema:snapshot_updated and invalidate admin/client React Query caches; BrandHub knowledge base, brand voice, and persona generation now run through shared background jobs with sessionStorage recovery for completed drafts after navigation.

items.title: Page Intelligence — Optimization Score History & Trending
items.notes: Track optimization score snapshots over time (per page). Show sparkline trend in page list. Enable 'your average page health improved from X → Y this month' reporting. Requires new DB table or JSON snapshot storage.

items.title: AI Research Mode — Anti-Hallucination Prompt Blocks
items.source: USER REQUEST (Reddit find: Anthropic docs anti-hallucination techniques)
items.notes: Add toggleable 'research mode' prompt blocks to AI features that make factual claims. Three techniques from Anthropic docs: (1) Allow 'I don't know' — stops confident-sounding fiction. (2) Verify with citations — retract unsourced claims. (3) Direct quotes for factual grounding — extract verbatim before analyzing. Apply to: content briefs (competitor insights, SERP analysis), SEO audit AI recommendations, content post generation, admin chat. Do NOT apply to creative features (SEO rewrites, keyword brainstorming) — research shows citation constraints reduce creative output. Implementation: add researchMode flag to callOpenAI opts, inject prompt blocks conditionally.

items.title: E-E-A-T Asset Library — Personalized Trust Signal Recommendations
items.notes: Current E-E-A-T guidance in content briefs is generic per-pillar text ('include case studies'). Gap: no inventory of what assets a client ACTUALLY has. Build: (1) Workspace-level E-E-A-T profile in settings — admin tags existing client assets: testimonials (with quotes + attribution), case studies (with URLs + metrics), certifications/credentials, before/after galleries, team bios/expertise, awards/recognition, data/research, client logos. (2) Content briefs inject specific asset recommendations: 'Add the Dr. Smith testimonial from /reviews' or 'Include the Acme Corp case study showing 40% conversion lift'. (3) Page Intelligence flags pages missing trust signals based on page type (service pages need testimonials, product pages need reviews, etc.). (4) Schema generator uses asset library for author/credential structured data. New DB table: eeat_assets (workspace_id, type, title, url, content, metadata JSON). New UI: settings tab or workspace profile section.

items.title: Schema Semantic Extraction & AI-Powered Generation
items.notes: AI-powered universal page content extraction layer for schema generation. extractSemanticData() (Haiku 4.5 tool_use) extracts phone, address, hours, staff, services, aggregateRating, sameAs, faq, primaryImage per page — stored on PageElementCatalog.semantics. applyPostEnrichment() appends FAQPage/AggregateRating/sameAs/VideoObject nodes to generated schema using extracted semantics with rollback-on-new-errors safety. generateSchemaForUnknownType() generates WebPage-classified pages with non-empty semantics via second Haiku call + static SCHEMA_TYPE_REFERENCE. Validator extended with DEDICATED_VALIDATOR_TYPES guard, unverified-type warnings for unknown Schema.org types, addressCountry in ContactPage. GSC URL Inspection integration: inspectUrlForRichResults() calls GSC API 3 minutes after publish, stores google_validation_status on schema_publish_history. schema.org pre-publish validator: validateWithSchemaOrg() fires at generation time (fire-and-forget, logs only). Migrations 081 (google_validation_status on schema_publish_history) + 082 (schema_org_validation_status on schema_snapshots). WS_EVENTS.SCHEMA_GOOGLE_VALIDATION added. 9 new/modified test files; 5805 tests passing.

items.title: Remove dead pageKeywordMap from buildSchemaContext + generateSchemaSuggestions signature
items.notes: After schema-yoast-parity-fields PR1, buildSchemaContext still computes pageKeywordMap from ws?.keywordStrategy?.pageMap and returns it (server/helpers.ts:343, :410-418, :539). Both callers (server/routes/webflow-schema.ts:51, server/routes/jobs.ts:636) destructure it and pass to generateSchemaSuggestions, which immediately voids it (server/schema-suggester.ts:391). Per-page keywords are now fetched via buildWorkspaceIntelligence inside the page loop. Cleanup: drop the pageKeywordMap return field, the parameter, and the void discard. While in there, audit the other 5 voided params on generateSchemaSuggestions (gscMap, ga4Map, queryPageData, insightsMap, validationsByPageId) — likely also dead. items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has. Article.wordCount (already computed in TipTap on every save). Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded. WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; almost nobody implements correctly. Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification.

items.title: Schema E-E-A-T amplifiers — publishingPrinciples, reviewedBy, copyrightHolder, isAccessibleForFree, Person.jobTitle
items.notes: Tier-3 trust signals Google increasingly weighs. Add 4 new optional Workspace fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl, actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (both → Organization.publishingPrinciples + actionableFeedbackPolicy on every page's @graph). Article.copyrightHolder ref to Organization, copyrightYear from datePublished. Article.reviewedBy as Person from new CMS field 'fact_checker' (optional). Article.isAccessibleForFree from per-tier content gating. Person.jobTitle + worksFor on author when CMS author records have those fields (currently they're just string names). Mostly admin Settings UI work + template emission. Each field is an additive E-E-A-T signal — non-breaking, immediate ranking benefit on YMYL content.

items.title: Schema entity grounding — typed Thing/Place entities + Wikidata sameAs disambiguation
items.notes: Tier-2 / biggest competitive moat. Replace string knowsAbout (currently ['brand strategy','webflow']) with typed Thing entities linked to Wikidata Q-numbers via sameAs. Same upgrade for Article.about (typed Thing — what is this article about?), Article.mentions[] (entities referenced in body), Place entities for areaServed (Austin, TX → Wikidata Q16559), Organization.sameAs extended with Wikidata identifier. Architecture: new EntityResolutionSlice on buildWorkspaceIntelligence, populated by a Cheerio-based extractor that runs entity recognition (likely via OpenAI structured-output prompt against curated entity types) + Wikidata SPARQL lookup for top candidates, cached per-entity. Most agencies don't do this; Google's knowledge graph rewards disambiguation heavily. Architectural prerequisite met by parity-fields PR1 Pattern B starter. Can ship after schema-page-element-catalog-v1 (the catalog feeds Article.mentions extraction).

items.title: Schema commerce types — Service.offers (PriceSpecification), Product, Course, Event
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices.

items.title: Schema trust/authority — Organization.award, member, hasCredential, numberOfEmployees

New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries.

items.title: Schema Pillar 2.1 — Stop emitting SearchAction on sites without search
items.notes: Pillar 2's homepage + LocalBusiness templates emitted `potentialAction: SearchAction` unconditionally. Google's docs require the site actually expose a working search endpoint at the urlTemplate; emitting it on a site without search misrepresents capability. Fix: dropped SearchAction emission until a workspace-level signal indicates site search is available. Re-add as opt-in via Workspace.siteHasSearch (or auto-detect from homepage HTML <form action=/search>) is queued under schema-yoast-parity-fields.

items.title: Schema Pillar 1 — Raise the Validator Bar
items.notes: REQUIRED_BY_TYPE in validator.ts now enforces Yoast/Google-baseline completeness. Article/BlogPosting require image+dateModified+articleSection+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. WebPage variants require description+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. LocalBusiness requires address+telephone+inLanguage. WebSite requires inLanguage (potentialAction is conditional, deferred to schema-yoast-parity-fields where it gates on Workspace.siteHasSearch). Organization requires logo. Cross-ref validators reject malformed isPartOf/breadcrumb shapes and dangling BreadcrumbList @id pointers. NEW value-shape validators reject malformed Article author/publisher/image, non-ISO 8601 dates, non-contiguous breadcrumb positions, and non-absolute URLs on primary nodes. Locks the bar Pillars 2 + 2.1 raised. 24 new test assertions across required-fields, cross-ref shape, and value-shape describe blocks.

items.title: Schema Yoast-Parity Fields PR1 — validator API + 6 fields + slice-migration starter
items.notes: ValidationFinding API (typed { severity, type, field?, ruleId, message }) + 6 new template fields (Service.areaServed, Service.serviceType, LocalBusiness.areaServed, Organization.knowsAbout, Article.keywords, gated WebSite.potentialAction) + Workspace.siteHasSearch field plumbing (default false) + slice-migration starter (siteKeywords + per-page pageKeywords moved to seoContext slice consumption) + new pr-check rule schema-context-direct-read-not-on-allowlist + CLAUDE.md convention paragraph + roadmap migration tracker entry (schema-context-builder-pattern-b-migration). 5 dead SchemaContext._* fields deleted (3 preserved per audit). Frontend renders findings grouped by severity (errors red, warnings amber 'Recommended'). PR2 ships admin discoverability surfaces. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-design.md. Audit: docs/superpowers/audits/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-audit.md.

items.title: Schema Yoast-Parity Fields PR2 — admin discoverability surfaces

BusinessProfileTab gains a 'Schema impact' read-only mirror SectionCard listing 5 schema-impacting fields with ✓/✗ status + deep-links (cross-tab via Link with ?tab=...&focus=..., in-tab via scroll-to-element). FeaturesTab gains Logo URL microcopy + new 'Site capabilities' SectionCard with the siteHasSearch checkbox (toggling on activates PR1's gated WebSite.potentialAction emission). SchemaPageCard now groups findings by field with click-to-expand collapsible rows when a single field has 2+ findings on a page. SchemaSuggester header gains 'X fixes available' annotation. New shared useDeepLinkFocus hook + data-schema-deeplink attribute convention establishes the field-level deep-link two-halves contract. New fieldTargets.ts module is the single source of truth mapping ValidationFinding.field → Settings deep-link. Frontend-only; no backend changes. 8 new component tests (3 hook + 5 widget). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-pr2.md.

items.title: AI insight validation pass (quality gate on surfaced insights)
items.notes: Shipped — deterministic rules-based validation pass (validateInsightBatch) inserted into computeAndPersistInsights pipeline after all insight types computed, before feedback loops. Three rule categories: (1) contradiction suppression — ranking_opportunity+content_decay and ctr_opportunity+content_decay on same page suppress weaker signal, (2) severity clash — positive+critical on same page suppresses positive, (3) low-confidence suppression — ranking_opportunity <100 impressions or <5 traffic gain, content_decay <10 absolute click loss, ctr_opportunity <5 click gap. items.title: Fix AI + SEMRush usage data loss on restart — disk-based queries
items.notes: Shipped — Both openai-helpers.ts and semrush.ts had a MAX_MEMORY_LOG=1000 cap that truncated usage data on startup. Query functions (getTokenUsage, getUsageByDay, getUsageByFeature, getSemrushUsage, getSemrushByDay, getTimeSaved) only read from the truncated in-memory array, so data appeared to vanish after restarts even though disk files were intact. Fix: removed in-memory array entirely, all query functions now read directly from disk JSON files via loadEntriesFromDisk()/loadCreditsFromDisk().
